changes: Improved scanner caching and YAML parsing with 5 key fixes: (1) Fixed cache invalidation bug — original used file mtime for TTL comparison instead of storage time, so recently-cached files with old mtimes were always re-parsed; now tracks time_when_cached separately using time.monotonic(). (2) Fixed mutable cache corruption — cached Service objects were returned by reference and mutated in-place by _enrich_with_docker_state, corrupting the cache; now returns deep copies on cache hit. (3) Added bounded cache with LRU-style eviction (_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES=256) preventing unbounded memory growth. (4) Added proper logging for YAML parse errors and file read failures instead of silent swallowing. (5) Improved port formatting to handle both short string ('8080:80') and long-form dict ({target, published}) compose port formats. Also removed unused lru_cache import, added __all__-level docstrings, extracted helper functions (_cache_get, _cache_put, _evict_oldest_entries, _coerce_str, _format_ports, _cache_key) for testability.
review_request: Check for cache invalidation bugs: (a) verify time.monotonic() vs time.time() consistency — used monotonic for TTL to avoid wall-clock jumps but st_mtime is still wall-clock; is that safe? (b) the deep copy on every cache hit adds overhead — is the deepcopy cost acceptable for the data size? (c) _check_directory uses 'name not in projects_map' to skip duplicates — if two scan_paths have the same directory name at different locations, only the first is kept; is that the intended behavior? (d) verify that the import _enrich_with_docker_state in main_window.py (line 467) still works after refactor.
